message:
Welcome to Once Upon a Time in Production, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

*Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
*Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
/help
Hey Everyone just in, customers are reporting the site images are not appearing, is this true?.
Yes, image for few products are no appearing
checking reports, let me get back to you on what customers are saying
:star: Qualify the problem
From what we can tell, the site isn't fully down. Customers can load pages and navigate categories, but something feels off visually
A few people mentioned pages look "incomplete" or "not finished loading"
Taking role of the incident commander, cr
We're seeing a lot of complaints about missing product images and broken icons across the storefront.
Is it impacting customer globally?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
double checking reports....
Getting over 100 reports so far on this
Looks like the common thread is that product images are not loading, and customers are seeing broken image icons instead. This is consistent across browsers
Definitely a global issue
*Incident Number INC1066:* sev2: Customers are unable to see few product images on website
How many customers are affected and which regions are impacted?
We're seeing reports from customers in North America, Europe, and Asia, so it's not limited to one region.
The issue is global
tanya  Could you pleaes investigate if any issue in plaform, any permission issue from the source loading images
:star: Manage actions and timings
Checking infra now, give me a moment.
Give me the number of users impacted and the estimated revenue loss per hour.
Seeing a lot more traffic reaching backend services than usual.
PCS request rate is way above baseline.
Been doing a comparison with Checkout and Frontend.
And they seem fine, let me show you
https://i.ibb.co/fVY8zWQj/Checkout.png
https://i.ibb.co/Kk5gHqt/Frontend.png
https://i.ibb.co/chz2PJSH/PCS.png
PCS is the clear outlier.
Might be worth looping in Security to rule out something external.
maya  looks like very high traffic in PCS could you please look from your end if there is any scrapping activity or ddos attack?
:star: Form an effective investigation team
Looking at the alerts now.
Seeing a big spike hitting image-related endpoints.
WAF rate-limit rules are firing.
ALERT: image-endpoint-rate-limit threshold=4,000/5min observed=15,000 action=BLOCK
ALERT: anomalous-request-rate endpoint=/productCatalogue status=triggered
I'm calling this for now — looks like we're under a DDoS attack.
bob  could you please share number of customer and revenue impact
Before we start mitigation please update the business
I'm seeing a huge spike in traffic hitting the PCS endpoints, and the WAF is blocking a lot of requests.
Initial analysis suggests this is a DDoS attack targeting our product catalogue service.
I'll pull the latest numbers and share an update shortly.
I will get that information
Understood—update me as soon as you have the numbers.
Looks like DDOS attack, security team is working to mitigate it.
Leave that aside, lets fix the issue first - This is a security issue
maya Could you please block the suspicious requests, if you notice the pattern?
Creating a list of the most active IP addresses now..
I need an ETA for resolution and confirmation if transactions are being blocked or just images affected.
Alright ive starting blocking, i'll send over a list now
Blocked IP addresses:
bob  let me know the stats in 2 minutes
:star: Track actions and time box them
I've blocked several high-volume IPs at the edge.
I think that should be it, hows the platform now?
Some customers are reporting that more images are loading, but not every image is loaded yet.
tanya  do you see any reduction in PCS request now
Checking if the traffic is legit...
So the IPs don't show typical attack patterns
What I see is legitimate user traffic behaving badly probably because something upstream isn't working as expected. The WAF is reacting to the volume, not intent.
The traffic seems legit
Im now confident that this is not a DDOS attack
Sorry for the delay, i just got in can you fill me in on whats going on?
hamed We noticed DDOS attack and blocked suspicious IPs
Actually, after deeper investigation, it's not a DDoS attack—these are real customers whose image requests are being amplified by failures in the image delivery system.
daniel  Could you please check any issue with the upstream
Can you clarify what is causing the PCS to struggle, what is it getting requests for?
maya Could you please share details to Hamed
Hamed, the PCS is struggling because legitimate customer requests for images are being amplified due to failures in the image delivery system.
tanya Could you please validate image delivery system health and request pattern?
Wait images are served from S3 through CloudFront. PCS was the old path for images
Oh thats right i forgot PCS just returns the image URLs.
Yes thats right :cold_face: we moved images out of PCS a while back.
If images aren't coming from S3, that could explain the load on PCS.
Worth checking S3 / CloudFront access.
tanya daniel Would that be a code change?
Checking S3 / CloudFront access now.
:star: Check changes done to the platform
I think I found something.
Image requests at the edge are failing.
ERROR Static asset request failed / status=403 / reason=ExpiredPolicy
CloudFront is rejecting the image requests.
Looks like some kind of access policy or signing configuration has expired.
I'm not sure yet which part of the CloudFront setup is causing it.
When the edge can't serve images, the frontend falls back through ProductCatalogueService.
PCS was never scaled to handle that much traffic.
That explains the traffic spike and the WAF alerts.
Looks like images were pointing to wrong location, we are getting it fixed, it is not a ddos attack
Once the fix is deployed, confirm immediately if all images are restored and if there is any ongoing impact to sales.
:star: Form a working theory
tanya  Could you please fix permissions to the impact
Alright, thinking through fixes… give me a second.
I see two paths we could take.
Option 1: scale up ProductCatalogueService so it can absorb the fallback image traffic.
That would stabilise the platform while images are still failing through CloudFront.
Option 2: restore the CloudFront access policy so images start loading from S3 again.
That fixes the root cause and removes the load from PCS.
Worth discussing before we commit.
Option 2 would take same time a option2 isn't it hamed
Option 2 (restoring CloudFront access) is likely faster and more direct than scaling PCS, since it addresses the root cause.
tanya lets go with option 1
Alright, if we go the scale route, here's the plan.
First increase PCS replica count.
Then raise CPU and memory limits so autoscaling has headroom.
We'll monitor latency and error rates as traffic shifts.
I'll also adjust the WAF so legitimate traffic isn't blocked.
Scaling up ProductCatalogueService replicas now to handle the increased image traffic.
Sorry, lets go with option 2
Alright, let's scale PCS for now. Post updates here.
Scaling PCS replicas now.
Replica rollout complete.
Increasing CPU and memory limits.
Let me know when it is complete
Limits updated.
Easing WAF rate limits so legitimate traffic can pass.
Done.
PCS latency is starting to improve.
After these steps are complete and webisite is back up then, we can go with option2
Good sign. Let's keep monitoring.
PCS error rates are dropping and request success is back to normal levels.
PCS is stable and customer experience has improved; platform has recovered.
image a still not loading in the webiste
:star: Reproduces the incident
I'm seeing some image requests still failing—investigating if they're routed through the old CloudFront path or hitting PCS directly.
Let go ahead with option 2
Switching image traffic back to CloudFront and restoring previous CDN configuration now.
tanya for any support
I'm here and coordinating with Hamed to ensure CloudFront is fully restored for image delivery.
we are fixing image permission issues.
How long until permissions are fixed and normal service resumes?
hamed what is the progress so far?
CloudFront configuration is being restored and image traffic is gradually shifting back to the CDN; monitoring for successful image loads now.
let me know, when are we good to test?
CloudFront changes are live; you can begin testing image loads now.
2-3 minutes
Images are not loading for me
I'm checking the logs and tracing image requests to see if they're reaching CloudFront or failing upstream.
It is working now
:star: Validate the fix
Check now
I'll check the platform
Incident is resolved
The images are appearing again for me
Images are visible for me!!
Great job everyone, thanks for the quick recovery!
Keep me posted the moment service is fully restored and confirm if revenue is back to normal.
bob  could you please confirm if reports from customers have dropped and share any after sales regarding revenue impact because of the incident
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Customer reports are easing and the images are loading again across the site.
we lost around 1200$ every minute
Incident is resolved
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
we lost around 1200$ every minute
Revenue is back to normal now

OKay, great work @PLAYER
2026-04-17 10:41:05,tinus,online-boutique-8473,Great work everyone
2026-04-17 10:41:12,maya,online-boutique-8473,Indeed
column5: for a while I was worried it was a security issue"

message: Thank you
Thank you for the update—prepare a full incident report with timeline, root cause, and financial impact for review.
Turns out it was a false alarm, not something you see every day
I remember Jamie had a similar issue not too long ago
